CMake toolchain file for building on Linux ARMv7l with specific compiler and architecture flags.

# Purpose
This configuration file is used to set up the build environment for a software project targeting an ARMv7 architecture on a Linux system. It specifies the system name as `Linux` and the processor type as `armv7l`. The C compiler is set to `arm-linux-gnueabihf-gcc`, which is a cross-compiler for ARM architecture. The file also defines common architecture flags under `FLAGS_COMMON_ARCH`, which include `-mthumb`, `-mfpu=vfp`, and `-march=armv7`. These flags configure the compiler to generate code for the ARMv7 architecture with VFPv3 floating-point support, using the d0-d15 registers. If changes are made to the floating-point unit settings, the `setjmp` and `longjmp` functions for ARMv7 must be updated accordingly.
